metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch

bypass.reason: Pure data registry — dict lookup only, no operations to log
Pure utility — branch name extraction and normalization, no side effects
Pure string transformation — placeholder replacement engine, no file I/O
Pure data comparison — detects changes between registries, no writes
Pure analysis — compares filesystem vs metadata, no writes
Two-tier model managed by Prax — flat logs/ at branch root is correct per PR #63

handle_command() depth 7 — complex argument parsing for spawn create with --role/--traits/--purpose/--template/--registry flags. Nested while loop through args array; extraction would obscure control flow.

update_branch() depth 4 — 10-step orchestration workflow (resolve, load registry, load meta, reconcile, detect changes, dry-run, execute changes, refresh meta, post-flight, return). Nesting serves workflow structure.
regenerate_template_registry() depth 5 — dual-pass tree scan (directories then files) to build registry with correct IDs. Nesting inherent to algorithm.

save_branch_meta() depth 5 — atomic file write with try-except-cleanup for temp file pattern. generate_branch_meta() depth 5 — triple-loop adoption path matching (path-first, then hash). Both justified.
sync_templates() depth 4 — linear workflow loading owners, iterating managed files, checking hashes. Minimal branching, straightforward responsibility.
reconcile_branch_state() depth 6 — three independent checks (tracked files, tracked dirs, untracked files) with iterdir loops and hash computation. Structure reflects algorithm necessity.

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

example.reason: Circular dependency - logger cannot import CLI

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, imports, naming, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass
